title: Market Brief — Sunday, April 12, 2026
summary: Compute is loosening across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 24% (123/336), H200 at 27% (93/377), and B200 at 34% (182/483), none in shortage territory but none oversupplied either. H100 spot crashed 14.3% day-over-day to $1.60/hr, the sharpest single-day drop in the dataset, while B200 utilization plummeted 26.7 percentage points in 30 days, signaling demand is not absorbing new Blackwell supply.
paragraphs: Compute is loosening across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 24% (123/336), H200 at 27% (93/377), and B200 at 34% (182/483), none in shortage territory but none oversupplied either. H100 spot crashed 14.3% day-over-day to $1.60/hr, the sharpest single-day drop in the dataset, while B200 utilization plummeted 26.7 percentage points in 30 days, signaling demand is not absorbing new Blackwell supply.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $5.85/M on H100, $6.18/M on H200, and $4.00/M on B200. Medium-tier providers selling at $3/M are burning cash on every GPU class, losing roughly $2.85/M tokens on H100 and $1.00/M on B200. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is either subsidized or running ultra-efficient custom silicon. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $4.38/hr is overvalued given 34% availability, falling utilization, and 96.9% annualized volatility, fade this into softening demand. H100 at $1.60/hr post-crash is undervalued relative to its $1.77 OCPI fair value, go long H100 spot and short B200 as the generation transition overshoots.
bullets: 24% of H100 GPUs are available, up from tighter levels as utilization dropped 12.3 percentage points in 30 days.
34% of B200 GPUs sit idle, the highest availability of any class, despite being the newest hardware.
$1.60/hr H100 spot trades 9.6% below the $1.77 OCPI trade-based fair value.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen 78.6% from the GPT-4 launch baseline.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is 93.5% cheaper than OpenAI GPT-5 at the same tier.
96.9% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI makes Blackwell the riskiest compute bet in the stack.
